hawkish_phrases: Monetary policy will need to continue to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
remaining domestic inflationary pressures are resolving more slowly
Services consumer price inflation has remained elevated
Annual private sector regular average weekly earnings growth picked up quite sharply
some indicators of inflation expectations have increased recently
dovish_phrases: Most indicators of UK near-term activity have declined
Bank staff expect GDP growth to have been weaker at the end of the year
sluggish demand and a weakening labour market
A gradual approach to removing monetary policy restraint remains appropriate
the labour market is broadly in balance
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to hold Bank Rate at 4.75%, with three members preferring a 25bp cut, and maintained a hawkish bias emphasizing that policy must remain restrictive for sufficiently long despite acknowledging weaker near-term activity.
